page_content:
## 4. Dr. Jafari (The Research Connection)  
**Focus:** Professional PI outreach; referencing Trevor as the "Safe" bridge.  
**Subject:** Medical School LOR Request & Update: Arian Moridani (Former Research Volunteer)  
Dear Dr. Jafari,  
I hope the semester is going well and that the Advanced Robotics and Manufacturing Lab continues to thrive.  
I’ve been reflecting on my 200 hoursin your lab lately. Beyond the technical contributions to the TVIM publication , I frequently think about the "on-the-ground" research skills I gained under your mentorship . Whether it was 3D printing components, maintaining lab equipment, or troubleshooting robotic systems, those experiences gave me a true sense of what research looks and feels like.  
Perhaps most importantly, the literature reviews I conducted helped me develop the ability to read and synthesize complex papers quickly—a skill that has been very valuable during my graduate studies.  
Since graduating from UNT (Cum Laude, 3.64 GPA) , I have been building on that foundation at Georgia Tech, where I am pursuing an M.S. in Computer Science (Machine Learning) . I am now applying to medical school with the goal of bringing that same research-oriented mindset—and my new skills in Computer Science and Machine Vision—into clinical practice.  
I would be honored if you would support my candidacy with a letter of recommendation. Because Trevor Exleyoversaw my day-to-day work, I have also reached out to him to provide an update on my progress. To assist you, I’ve attached my updated CVand a summary of my recent work .  
I’ve set up an Interfolioaccount so you only need to upload your letter once for all my applications. Thank you for your time and continued mentorship.  
Best regards,  
Arian Moridani  
arianmoridani@my.unt.edu  
arian.moridani@outlook.com
---
